subject: Statistics
title: The Friendship Paradox
description: Learn The Friendship Paradox in one minute.

hook.text: On average, your friends have more friends than you do. This is mathematically guaranteed, not just bad luck.

buildup.text: Popular people appear in many friend lists. You're more likely to be friends with someone who has many friends.

discovery.text: It's a sampling bias: high-degree nodes are overrepresented when you sample through connections.

twist.text: The paradox extends: your co-authors publish more, your partners have more exes, your followers have more followers.

climax.text: It's used in public health: vaccinating friends of random people targets well-connected individuals efficiently.

punchline.text: Your friends are more popular than you. It's not personal — it's math.

quiz.question: Why do your friends tend to have more friends than you?
options: Popular people appear in many friend lists, so they're overrepresented in your sample of friends
